observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require:
a. The Municipal Accountant and Municipal General Services Officer to reconcile
the discrepancies noted between the RPCPPE and accounting records and
effect the adjustments to arrive at reconciled balances, and to maintain
updated PCs and PPELCs as basis for reconciliation and validation;
b. The Municipal Accountant to draw a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to correctly
classify the Construction in Progress – Infrastructure Assets amounting to
P13,057,098.45 as PPE instead of Investment Property;
c. The Municipal Accountant to compute for the net book value of the small
value items totaling P2,919,865.00 recorded as PPE and draw a JEV to drop
these items from the books by debiting Prior Years’ Adjustment and
Accumulated Depreciation and by crediting the respective PPE accounts;
d. The Municipal Accountant to draw a JEV to recognize the unrecorded PPE
items totaling P6,513,600.00 by debiting the respective PPE accounts and by
crediting Prior Year’s Adjustment and the related Accumulated Depreciation;
and
e. The Municipal Accountant to reclassify the PPE totaling P12,841,589.38 to
appropriate PPE accounts according to its classification, specification and
nature.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to:
a. Submit a detailed schedule of the Other Receipts and Other Payments as
presented in the Statement of Cash Flows;
b. Submit a reconciliation statement for the cash flows under the Trust Fund and
trace the source of variance under the General Fund – Proper; and
c. Strictly observe the provisions of Paragraph 29 of IPSAS No. 2 and Paragraph
127 (c) of IPSAS No. 1 for the disclosure requirements regarding the Cash
Flow in the Notes to Financial Statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require:
a. The Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer to reconcile the
discrepancies noted between the actual amounts presented in the SCBAA
and SAAOB;
b. The Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer to provide complete
and accurate reconciliation between the actual amounts presented in the
SCBAA and the Statement of Financial Performance; and
c. The Municipal Accountant to include in the Notes to Financial Statements any
other relevant disclosures pertaining to the budget information of the
Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the concerned officials to
coordinate with the Provincial Government of Laguna as regards the imposition
of Sangguniang Panglalawigan Resolution No. 367 S. 2017 dated September
2017.

We also recommended that Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to
reconcile its records against the records of the Provincial Government of Laguna
to validate the amount of Interest Payable recorded in the Financial Statements
and to make the necessary adjustment in the books of the Municipality to correct
the variance and to derecognize the Interest Payable in the books if warranted by
the imposition of the Sangguniang Panglalawigan Resolution 367 S. 2017.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor advise the Municipal Accountant to
facilitate the request for write-off to clean the dormant receivables in the books of
account in accordance with COA Circular No. 2016-005 and to reflect reliable
amount of receivables in the Financial Statements. Also, continue to exert more
effort for its collectability when circumstances would warrant.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the concerned Officials and the
Municipal Accountant to assess its depreciable assets valued at P312,504,272.35
and comply with the requirement of Section 26, 52, and 53 of IPSAS No. 21 for
recognition of any Impairment to adjust any overstatement/understatement of the
Property, Plant and Equipment account reflected in the Financial Statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor comply with the provision of COA
Circular No. 94-013 dated December 13, 1994 and EO No. 431 dated May 30,
2005 and direct the Municipal Accountant to transfer/return the unutilized balances
of Due to NGAs to enable the Source Agencies to utilize these funds for other
priority programs and projects of the Agency and to clear the unliquidated Due to
NGAs in the books.

Also direct the concerned officials for immediate implementation and utilization
of the trust fund received so as not to deprive the intended beneficiaries of the
benefits to be derived therefrom.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor to direct the concerned officials to
ask the source agencies for the Memoranda of Agreement (MOA)/Trust
Agreement/Memorandum of Understanding for the above mentioned fund
transfers to provide bases in the utilization of funds and disposition of any
balance thereof upon completion of the purpose of the funds transferred.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to
immediately submit all the lacking supporting documents on the payments of
welfare goods amounting to P11,287,801.05 and justifications for allowing the
payments thereof despite incomplete documentation.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to execute Contract Agreement for the
services of Waste disposal for legal protection of the Municipality and comply
with the documentary requirements of submitting contract before effecting
payment.

Also, submit to the Auditor the perfected and approved contract for evaluation as
provided for in Section 3.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-001 to ascertain the
propriety and regularity of the transactions.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to
immediately submit all the lacking supporting documents on the liquidation of
Cash Advances drawn for the purchase of welfare goods and meals amounting to
P2,104,000.00 and provide justifications for allowing the liquidation thereof
despite incomplete documentation.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Local Development Council
to maximize the implementation of the development projects under the 20 per
cent Development Fund and enjoin the immediate utilization and implementation
of the remaining unimplemented projects in order to fully achieve the 100 per
cent realization and completion of development projects.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the concerned Municipal
officials to ensure that disbursement out of the fund are those that pertains only to
disaster risk and management activities provided under Joint Memorandum
Circular No. 2013-1.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Disaster Risk
Reduction and Management Officer (MDRRMO) to fully utilize the LDRRM Fund
to strengthen its capacity to mitigate disaster risk in order to ensure and maintain
the maximum implementation of disaster mitigation and preparedness
programs/projects/activities (PPAs), under the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and
Management Fund (LDRRMF) to provide optimum benefits to the constituents of
the locality in times of disaster.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Budget Officer to
appropriate at least five per cent of the approved Annual Budget for GAD in
compliance with Item 4.1 C.3 of the PCW-DILG-DBM NEDA JMC No. 2013-001.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the GAD Focal Point Designate
to monitor the strict implementation of plans, programs and activities included in
the GPB and fully utilize the GAD Budget to achieve its goal of promoting gender-
responsive governance, protecting and fulfilling women’s human rights and
promoting women’s empowerment.

We also recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct the GAD Focal Point
Designate and the Municipal Planning and Development Officer to consider the
current COVID-19 pandemic situation in the preparation of budgeted plans,
programs and activities under GAD Fund to ensure that PPAs will be fully
implemented.

observations.managementAction: The results of the physical count conducted by the Municipality’s GSO on existing PPE as of December 31, 2021 were communicated with the Accounting Office, and reconciliation for the inventory and accounting reports.
observations.reason: The erroneous recording of Construction in Progress – Investment Property, as well as the difference in classification of certain equipment such as welding machine contributed to the variance between the accounting and property records.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the balance of RPT Receivables and SET Receivables and reconcile the balances of RPT Receivables and SET Receivables in order to reflect accurate and reliable balances in the Financial Statements.
observations.managementAction: A Journal Entry Voucher was drawn by the Accounting Office to recognize adjustments noted, reconciled the balance of the RPT and SET receivables in the Financial Statements.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the Provincial Accountant to reconcile the balances of Due from LGU and Due to LGU appearing in the records of the Provincial Government and to make the necessary adjustment to arrive at an accurate and correct balance in the Financial Statements.
observations.managementAction: The necessary adjustments have also been made to reflect the correct balance of the receivable and payable accounts in the Financial Statements.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to:
a. submit a detailed schedule of the Other Receipts and Other Disbursements as presented in the Statement of Cash Flows; and
b. henceforth, strictly observe the provisions of Paragraphs 1 and 27 of IPSAS No. 2 and Paragraph 127 (c) of IPSAS No. 1 for the disclosure requirements regarding the Cash Flow in the Notes to Financial Statements.
observations.managementAction: The Accounting Office has submitted a breakdown of the Other Receipts and Other Disbursements the Office of the Auditor as required.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to include a full disclosure of the Municipality’s LDRRMF in the Notes to Financial Statements to comply with the provisions of Paragraphs 21 and 127 (c) of IPSAS No. 1.
observations.managementAction: The available appropriation, utilization and unexpended balance of the LDRRMF, and Trust Liabilities – DRRM in the Trust Fund books were disclosed in the Municipality’s Notes to Financial Statements.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and other concerned officials to:
a. comply with COA Circular No. 2016-005 to accurately reflect reliable amount of receivables in the Financial Statements; b. consider requesting for authority to write-off dormant receivables subject to the conditions set forth by the circular and submit to the Auditor for review and evaluation; c. set up Allowance for Impairment for recognition of possible losses and effect the adjustment in the amount of asset reflected in the Financial Statements for fair presentation; and d. still continue to exert effort for its collectability when circumstances would warrant.
observations.managementAction: An Allowance for Impairment for the long overdue receivables was set up to recognize possible losses caused by the inability to collect outstanding dues. The Accounting Office has prepared an ageing schedule of loans receivables to be able to set up an allowance for impairment to recognize possible losses in the Financial Statements.
observations.reason: From 31 to 78 days in CY 2020, delays in the release of documentation and inability to reassess the actual collectability of the dormant receivables caused by the limited interactions under the changing Alert Systems during the spread of new variants of the COVID-19 pandemic.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) Secretariat to comply and adhere to the rules and regulations set forth in RA No. 9184 or the Government Procurement Reform Act and its Revised IRR as regards the submission of required documentation in the procurement of Infrastructure Projects so as to promote transparency, accountability, equity, efficiency, and economy in the procurement process.
observations.managementAction: The Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) was able to attach printed copies of posting of the Notice of Award to the Philippine Government Electronic Procurement System (PhilGEPS) in some of their projects submitted to the Auditor’s Office. It was, however, noted that the printed copies of posting the Notice of Proceed and the Contract to the same portal were not included. The BAC reasoned out that this was due to system maintenance, upgrades and most of the time, difficulties and technicalities encountered in the PhilGEPS website.
observations.reason: System maintenance and technical difficulties encountered in the PhilGEPS portal prevented complete posting of all required documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the General Services Office (GSO) to cause the disposal of the property reported as unserviceable in the IIRUP in accordance with Section 79 of PD No. 1445 and Section 170 to 180 of the Manual on the Rules and Procedures on the Management of Property for Local Government Units (LGUs). Upon disposal, direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare Journal Entry Voucher in order to derecognize these properties and adjust the overstatement of PPE account in the books as required under IPSAS No. 17.
observations.managementAction: Based on the Report on Physical Count of Property Procured and Inventory as of December 31, 2021, the GSO has partially disposed of the unserviceable properties. The Municipality has already coordinated with HMR Envirocycle Philippines Inc., as early as March 4, 2021 for the safe disposal of hazardous components, and secured a Certificate of Assured Destruction. The Accounting Office has drawn a JEV to derecognize all disposed properties as of December 31, 2021, based on the report of the GSO.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to be able to release the barangay shares from RPTI within five days after the end of each quarter as required under Section 271(d) of RA No. 7160.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant have coordinated to be able to release the barangay shares from RPTI within the prescribed date of transfer as required by RA No. 7160.
observations.reason: No reason was provided for the inability to fully implement the recommendation in CY 2021.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Administrator and the General Services Officer to facilitate the titling of the parcels, as well as the transfer of titles that are still in process, in compliance with Section 148 of COA Circular No. 92-386 and Section 47 of the IRR of the Local Government Code of 1991 to avoid contingent claims and third-party disputes.
observations.managementAction: The General Services Offices, with the assistance of the Municipal Assessor and Municipal Legal Officer exerted efforts to establish the proper ownership of the remaining lands affected the time period of processing the titling and transfer of titles of the subject lots.
observations.reason: The titling of the parcels is still in continuous process; alternative work arrangements by the different government offices affected the time period for processing the titling.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the General Services Office to prepare the list of insurable property and facilitate the filing/application for the necessary insurance coverage of all these assets with the GSIS under the Municipal General Insurance Fund as required under RA No. 656, as amended and COA Circular No. 92-390.
observations.managementAction: Copies of documents required by the Government Service Insurance System (GSIS) such as the application form, map, building floor plan, tax declaration, etc. for five buildings: New Municipal Building, Escolapia, Old Municipal Building, Isolation Facility and Home for the Aged (last two buildings are in Barangay Jasaan) were already submitted to them.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the members of the LCPC to submit Accomplishment Report and the LCPC Accomplishment Report and the utilization report for review and evaluation.
observations.managementAction: The MSWDO has submitted a copy of the LCPC Accomplishment Report and the Report on the LCPC Fund utilization for audit. An Annual Liquidation Report for CY 2021 was also submitted, which showed 75.42 per cent utilization or P1,895,232.00 of the net programmed authorization for the year amounting to P2,512,964.72.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Local Development Council to maximize the utilization of implementation of the development projects under the 20 percent Development Fund and enjoin the immediate utilization and implementation of the remaining unimplemented projects in order to fully achieve the objectives of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: Audit of Local Development Fund for CY 2021 disclosed P39,688,119.81 or 70.97 per cent of the total appropriation of P55,918,618.80 as of December 31, 2021. The Management explained that the remaining unutilized balance are for on-going PPAs that were completed in the first quarter of CY 2022.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office (MDRRMO) to prepare the Inventory List of Procured/Donated Items for DRRM and submit to the General Services Office for proper recording and monitoring in compliance with the provisions of MNGAS for LGUs.
observations.managementAction: The MDRRMO has provided the GSO with listings for reference in recording available and issued supplies and equipment.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office (MDRRMO) to prepare the Inventory List of Procured/Donated Items for DRRM and submit the same to the Auditor for reference and monitoring in compliance with COA Circular No. 2014-02.
observations.managementAction: The MDRRMO submitted the Utilization Report to the Office of the Auditor for reference in audit.
observations.reason: Although there was a Utilization Report submitted, the report presented only per item expenditure and does not specify the inventory and PPE items procured and donated.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office (MDRRMO) to continue optimizing the utilization of the Mitigation Fund and further strengthen its capacity to mitigate disaster risk in order to ensure and maintain the maximum implementation of disaster mitigation and preparedness programs/projects/activities (PPAs), under LDRRMF.
observations.managementAction: With regards to the 80 per cent LDRRM Fund utilization, the MDRRMO explained that the spending rate dropped due to the immediate availability of appropriations that came from other funds, the Bayanihan fund in particular, that augmented the COVID-19 Prevention and Mitigation Programs’ expenditures and served as the main source of fund during the heightened alert due to pandemic. Balances that are transferred to the Trust Fund came from Capital Outlay (CO) PPAs. The Management committed to continue formulating policy, plans and programs to continuously utilize the LDRRM Fund in an efficient manner.
observations.reason: The immediate availability of appropriations from other sources such as the Bayanihan Fund augmented the COVID-19 related PPAs that were already budgeted to the current year’s LDRRMF, hence utilization rate appeared lower.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the GAD Focal Person to strictly adhere to Item V of COA Circular No. 2014-001 dated March 18, 2014 for the timely submission of the GAD Plan and Budget and GAD Accomplishment Report to the COA.
observations.managementAction: The Auditor’s Office was furnished with a copy of the GAD Accomplishment Report on February 4, 2022 which was one day ahead of the deadline per Item V of COA Circular No. 2014-001 dated March 18, 2014. The GAD Plan and Budget was submitted when requested by the Audit Team.
observations.reason: Although the GAD Accomplishment Report was submitted within the prescribed time, the GAD Plan and Budget was submitted only when requested by the Audit Team, which was beyond the deadline.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the GAD Focal Person to:
a. submit the GAD Accomplishment Report supported with the brief summary of the reported program or project, copies of reported policy issuances, results of HGDG tests and actions taken by the LGU on the COA audit findings and recommendations in compliance with Section C.8 (5) of the JMC No. 2013-01 of the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA; and
b. submit the documents that will show proof of the conduct of activities for proper determination of the success in addressing gender issues.
observations.managementAction: The GAD Accomplishment Report submitted to the Auditor’s Office was supported with brief summary of the reported PPAs, pictures to show the conduct of each of the GAD activities, duly signed listing of participants/ recipients and utilization report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the MDRRMO Property Officer to prepare the Inventory List of Procured/Donated Items for DRRM and submit the same to the Audit Team for monitoring in compliance with COA Circular No. 2014-02.
observations.managementAction: The MDRRMO Property Officer explained that they continuously update the records of the procured items for the DRRM. He also mentioned that with regards to the list of recipients, the Municipal Social Welfare and Development Office (MSWDO) and the Office of the Municipal Administrator have available records because they facilitate the distribution of relief goods.
observations.reason: The MDRRMO was not fully aware of the submission requirements to the Office of the Auditor.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require:
a. The Municipal Accountant and Municipal General Service Officer to reconcile the
discrepancies noted between the RPCPPE and the Accounting records
and effect the adjustment to arrive at reconciled balances and to maintain
updated PEC and PPELCs as basis for reconciliation and validation.
b. The Municipal Accountant to draw a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to drop the small
value property items from the books by debiting Prior Years’ Adjustment of
P81,127.67 and Accumulated Depreciation of P1,395,544.53 and by crediting the
respective PPE accounts of P1,476,672.20.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to
reconcile the balance of RPT Receivables and SET Receivables in order to reflect
accurate and reliable balances in the Financial Statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to
coordinate with the Provincial Accountant and reconcile the balance of Due from LGU
and Due to LGU appearing in the books of the Municipality against the balances of their
respective reciprocal accounts appearing in the records of the Provincial Government.

We also recommend that the necessary adjustment be made upon reconciliation to
correct the discrepancy to arrive at an accurate and correct balance of the account in the
Financial Statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to:
a. submit a detailed schedule of the Other Receipts and Other Disbursements as
presented in the Statement of Cash Flows; and
b. henceforth, strictly observe the provisions of Paragraphs 1 and 27 of IPSAS No. 2 and
Paragraph 127 (c) of IPSAS No. 1 for the disclosures requirement regarding the Cash
Flow in the Notes to Financial Statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to
include a full disclosure of the Municipality’s LDRRMF in the Notes to Financial
Statements to comply with the provisions of Paragraphs 21 and 127 (c) of IPSAS
No. 1.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal
Accountant in coordination with other concerned officials to initiate action in
cleaning the dormant receivables in the books of accounts in accordance with
COA Circular No. 2016-005. Also, direct the Municipal Accountant and other
official concerned to:
a. comply with COA Circular No. 2016-005 to accurately reflect reliable amount of
receivables in the Financial Statements;
b. consider requesting for authority to write-off dormant receivables subject to the
conditions set forth by the above circular and submit to the Auditor for review and
evaluation;
c. set up Allowance for Impairment for recognition of possible losses and effect the
adjustment in the amount of asset reflected in the Financial Statements for fair
presentation; and
d. still continue to exert effort for its collectability when circumstances would
warrant.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the BAC Secretariat to
comply and adhere to the rules and regulations set forth in RA No. 9184 or the
Government Procurement Reform Act and to its Revised IRR as regards the
submission of required documentation in the procurement of Infrastructure Projects so
as to promote transparency, accountability, equity, efficiency, and economy in the
procurement process.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the GSO to cause the disposal of
property reported as unserviceable in the IIRUP in accordance with Section 79 of PD
No. 1445 and Section 170 to 180 of the Manual on the Rules and Procedures on the
Management of Property for Local Government Units to decongest the area where these
properties were stockpiled and recognize possible income from the disposal thereof.
Upon disposal, direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare Journal Entry Voucher in
order to derecognize these properties and adjust the overstatement of PPE account in
the books as required under IPSAS No. 17.

We reiterated our audit recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the
Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to release the barangays’ shares
from RPTI within five days after the end of each quarter as required under Section
271(d) of RA No. 7160.

We reiterated our audit recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct the
Municipal Administrator and the General Services Officer to facilitate the titling of
the subject lots, as well as the transfer of titles that are still in process, in
compliance with Section 148 of COA Circular No. 92-386 and Section 47 of the
IRR of the Local Government Code of 1991 to avoid contingent claims and third-
party disputes.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal
General Services Officer to prepare the list of insurable property and facilitate the
filing/application for the necessary insurance coverage of all these assets with the
GSIS under the General Insurance Fund as required under RA No. 656, as
amended and COA Circular No. 92-390 dated November 17, 1992.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the members of the LCPC to
submit Accomplishment Report and the Report on the LCPC Fund utilization for
evaluation and determination as to whether the objectives on the promotion of
rights and welfare of the children in the community were fully achieved.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Local Development Council to
maximize the implementation of the development projects under the 20 percent
Development Fund and enjoin the immediate utilization and implementation of the
remaining unimplemented projects in order to fully achieve the objectives of the fund
for the benefit of the constituents of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Disaster Risk
Reduction and Management Office (MDRRMO) Property Officer to prepare the
Inventory List of Procured/Donated Items for DRRM and submit to the General
Services Office for proper recording and monitoring, in compliance with the
provisions of MNGAS for LGUS, Volume I.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Disaster Risk
Reduction and Management Officer (MDRRMO) Property Officer to prepare the
Inventory List of Procured/Donated Items for DRRM and submit the same to the
Audit Team for monitoring in compliance with COA Circular No. 2014-02.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Disaster Risk
Reduction and Management Officer (MDRRMO) to continue optimizing the
utilization of the Mitigation Fund and further strengthen its capacity to mitigate
disaster risk in order to ensure and maintain the maximum implementation of
disaster mitigation and preparedness programs/projects/activities (PPAs), under
LDRRMF.

We reiterated our audit recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct the GAD
Focal Person to strictly adhere to Item V of COA Circular No. 2014-001 dated
March 18, 2014 for the timely submission of the GAD Plan and Budget and GAD
Accomplishment Report to the COA.

We reiterated our audit recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct the GAD
Focal Person to:
a. submit the GAD Accomplishment Report supported with the brief summary of
the reported program or project, copies of reported policy issuances, results of
HGDG tests and actions taken by the LGU on the COA audit findings and
recommendations in compliance with Section C.8 (5) of the JMC No. 2013-01 of
the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA for evaluation; and
b. submit the documents that will show proof of the conduct of activities for
proper determination of the success in addressing gender issues.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to set up SET Receivables and its contra account Deferred SET based on submitted RPT assessed value and to reconcile the balances of the RPT reciprocal account and contra account RPT at year end. Effect the necessary adjustments in order to reflect accurate and reliable balances in the Financial Statements.
observations.managementAction: The Accounting Office has drawn the necessary Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to book up the Municipality’s SET Receivables, as well as its contra-account, Deferred SET.
observations.reason: Prior years’ balances were reconciled but still variances which need to be acted upon.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated the recommendation that the Municipal Mayor advise the Municipal Accountant to initiate action in cleaning the dormant receivables in the books of account in accordance with COA Circular 2016-005. Also, direct the Municipal Accountant and other official concerned to: a. comply with COA Circular No. 2016-005 to accurately reflect reliable amount of receivables in the Financial Statements; b. consider requesting for authority to write-off dormant receivables subject to the conditions set forth by the above circular and submit to the Auditor for review and evaluation; c. set up Allowance for Impairment for recognition of possible losses and effect the adjustment in the amount of asset reflected in the Financial Statements for fair presentation; and d. still continue to exert effort for its collectability when circumstances would warrant.
observations.managementAction: The Accounting Office has planned of reassessing the collectability of the dormant receivables and will be in-charge in the preparation of documents to support the request for write-off however, due to Pandemic and the implementation of Alternative Work Arrangement the activity was not completed.
observations.reason: The Management created a committee that will act on the preparation of documents to support the request for write-off however, due to Pandemic and the implementation of the Alternative Work Arrangement the activity was not completed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a. direct the Municipal Accountant to maintain property ledger cards to support the amount reflected in the general ledger to ascertain the completeness of the Inventory balances as reported in the Financial Statements; and b. require the General Service Officer to update the stock cards to fully account and validate the receipts and issuances of Inventories; and to conduct inventory taking and prepare Report of Physical Count of Inventory for reconciliation with the Property and Accounting records.
observations.managementAction: The Accounting Office started to maintain records for the inventories of the Municipality. The GSO continued to maintain records for the inventories of the Municipality.
observations.reason: Due to the adoption of alternative work arrangements under the pandemic and the Municipality’s electronic system for accounting is not yet fully operational the office lacked manpower to consistently update and monitor the ledger cards.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the Provincial Accountant and reconcile the balance of Due from LGUs account appearing in the books of the Municipality against the balance of Due to LGUs account appearing in the records of the Provincial Government. Effect the necessary adjustment upon reconciliation to correct the discrepancy to arrive at an accurate and correct balance of the account in the Financial Statements.
observations.managementAction: The Accountant coordinated with the Provincial Office of the Provincial Accountant to reconcile the records of both offices.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to draw the necessary Journal Entry Voucher to derecognize/drop the small value property items from the books by debiting Prior Years’ Adjustment and crediting the PPE accounts in the books in the amount of P234,027.51.
observations.managementAction: The Accounting Office has drawn the necessary Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to derecognize/drop the small tangible items costing below P10,000.00 and reclassified these as semi-expendable properties in the books.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated the recommendation that the Municipal Mayor: a. require the Municipal Engineer to comply with the reporting guidelines on Local Roads Asset Management System as prescribed under COA Circular No. 2015-008 and maintain subsidiary ledger and provide full disclosures of the details of Road Network in the Notes to Financial Statements using the prescribed format; b. provide the Municipal Accountant and Municipal General Services Officer with the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects; and c. MGSO to keep complete Road Network Property Cards.
observations.managementAction: The Accounting Office has provided additional disclosures for the Local Road Networks. Coordination among the Accounting Office, Municipal Engineering Office and General Services Office was undertaken to ensure that updates on the road networks are reflected by both the Accounting Office and the GSO’s respective reports. The MGSO committed to maintain complete Road Network Property Cards.
observations.reason: The errors encountered in the electronic system also affected the maintenance of records of Road Networks.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Administrator and the General Services Officer to facilitate the titling of the subject lots, as well as the transfer of titles that are still in process, in compliance with Section 148 of COA Circular No. 92-386 and Section 47 of the IRR of the Local Government Code of 1991 to avoid contingent claims and third-party disputes.
observations.managementAction: The Management committed to continuously monitor the process of the transfer of title for subject lots.
observations.reason: The adoption of alternative work arrangement by the government offices affected the time period of processing the titling and transfer of the subject titles.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal General Services Officer to prepare the list of insurable property and facilitate the filing/application for the necessary insurance coverage of all these assets with the GSIS under the General Insurance Fund as required under RA No. 656, as amended and COA Circular No. 92-390 dated November 17, 1992.
observations.managementAction: The Management explained that they are still waiting for the completion of documents such as the floor plans, etc., for submission to the GSIS as requirement for the application for the necessary insurance coverage.
observations.reason: Required documents for GSIS application (floor plans etc.) were not yet completed to date.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a. through the Sangguniang Bayan, establish specific guidelines, aligned with DSWD MC No. 11 series of 2019, in the granting of financial assistance to institutionalize a uniform schedule, consistent, objective system of operation in responding to cases of individuals and families in crisis situation; and b. direct the Accountant to submit to the Auditor the documents used as basis to the Office of the Auditor to support the payment of Financial Assistance to beneficiaries listed in Appendix M in order to prove the regularity of payment.
observations.managementAction: The Management has provided the Audit Team with a list of submitted documents that will support the payment of Financial Assistance made to the beneficiaries.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to adopt measures and strategies that will intensify and maximize collection of taxes. Likewise, avail of remedies and sanctions as provided in RA No. 7160 for strict imposition of collection that will generate income and increase the sources of fund of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The Treasury Office exerted efforts to work on the generation of reports as basis for release of the Barangays' shares in RPT. The Municipality also submitted list of delinquent properties with tax delinquency to the Provincial Treasury Office for further action.
observations.reason: The Management explained that collections were affected by the halt of economic activities due to COVID-19 and that issuance of warrants of levy by the Provincial Treasury discretionarily; the last issuance for taxpayers of the Municipality was already four years ago.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to release the barangays’ shares from RPT within five days after the end of each quarter as required under Section 271(d) of RA No. 7160.
observations.managementAction: The Treasury Office exerted efforts to work on the generation of reports as basis for release of the Barangays' shares in RPT despite the system errors that are encountered and the limited number of personnel working it due to the adoption of alternative work arrangement. They also added that five days is not enough for timely remittances.
observations.reason: System errors were encountered and the limited number of personnel working due to the adoption of alternative work arrangement hampered the implementation of the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to ensure that disbursement are processed and paid with complete Payment Vouchers in compliance with Section 9.1.3.2 of COA Circular 2012-001.
observations.managementAction: The Accounting Office has submitted the necessary documentation that will support the payment of various transactions to the Office of the Auditor.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to submit immediately the lacking documents for the paid Disbursement Vouchers in compliance with Section 9.1.3.2 of COA Circular 2012-001.
observations.managementAction: The Management has provided the Audit Team the submitted documents to support the payments for garbage disposal services in compliance with PD No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 2012-001.
observations.reason: Some of the required supporting documents are still for completion and submission.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Local Development Council to maximize the implementation of the development projects under 20 percent Development Fund and enjoin the immediate utilization and implementation of the remaining unimplemented projects in order to fully achieve the objectives of the fund for the benefit of the constituents of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Engineer and the Municipal Planning and Development Officer explained that a huge portion of the unutilized balance pertained to the Waste Treatment Facility project proposed for the Municipal Market. However, upon the proposal of an investment of a Public-Private Partnership with the SM Group, the Municipality opted to consider the option instead. Along the way of planning, the project did not materialize and was aborted, resulting to a huge chunk of unimplemented amount. The Municipality will resume the implementation of this project in Calendar Year 2021.
observations.reason: A huge percentage of the total cost pertained to the Waste Treatment Facility project which did not materialize as Public-Private Partnership and thus remained unimplemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the GAD Focal Person to strictly adhere to Item V of COA Circular No. 2014-001 dated March 18, 2014 for the timely submission of the GAD Plan and Budget and GAD Accomplishment Report to the COA.
observations.managementAction: The GAD Focal Person explained that she was the newly assigned GAD Focal Person and was not fully aware of the submission of reports to the COA Office. Nevertheless, she committed to comply with the timely submission of reports.
observations.reason: The newly-assigned GAD Focal Person lacked full awareness of the time period for submission of copies to the Office of the Auditor.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the GAD Focal Person to: a. submit the GAD Accomplishment Report supported with the brief summary of the reported program or project, copies of reported policy issuances, results of HGDG tests and actions taken by the LGU on the COA audit findings and recommendations in compliance with Section C.8 (5) of the JMC No. 2013-01 of the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA for evaluation; and b. submit the documents that will show proof of the conduct of activities for proper determination of the success in addressing gender issues.
observations.managementAction: The GAD Focal Person explained that she was not fully aware of all the necessary documentation to support the GAD Accomplishment Report. Nevertheless, the Management committed to comply with the audit recommendations.
observations.reason: Other necessary supporting documents were not provided due to lack of full awareness of the documentation requirements; Management provided some documents to DILG but was not aware COA must be furnished with same.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct the GAD Focal Point Person to establish a GAD database in compliance with Item 4.1 B of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA to serve as basis in budgeting and planning GAD PPAs which are responsive to the gender issues in the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality used the data gathered under the Community-Based Monitoring System (CBMS) as the Municipality’s GAD Database.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer (MDRRMO) to continue optimizing the utilization of the Mitigation Fund and further strengthen its capacity to mitigate disaster risk in order to ensure and maintain the maximum implementation of disaster mitigation and preparedness programs/projects/activities (PPAs), under LDRRMF.
observations.managementAction: The Management was amenable with the audit recommendation and committed to comply with it.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a. direct the MENRO to fully comply with the provisions cited in RA No. 9003 in order to effectively realize its goals on solid waste management for the well-being of its constituents; b. require all the 20 Barangays to establish their own MRF and to look for conducive locations to ensure environmentally-sound methods in solid waste management; c. encourage and support the establishment of Multi-Purpose Environment Cooperatives or cooperative Association to help promote the implementation of RA No. 9003; and d. direct the MSWMB and the MENRO to review and update the Ten-Year Solid Waste Management Plan of the Municipality every two years or as the need arises in compliance with Section 4 (g), Rule VI of the Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA No. 9003.
observations.managementAction: The Management used programs such as “BasuESkoLar - Basura Mo, Edukasyon Ko Solid Waste Program” and included the recommendation in the list of agenda during the Municipality’s first quarter meeting and forwarded the matter to the Sangguniang Bayan. The Barangays had established their own MRFs in compliance with RA No. 9003. The Management proposed meetings among junk shop owners to discuss effective solid waste management.
observations.reason: Some barangays lacked funds or space to build MRFs; community protocols and limitations hindered establishment of Multi-Purpose Environment Cooperatives and full updating/implementation of SWM Plan.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant
to set up SET Receivables and its contra account Deferred SET at the
beginning of the year based on submitted RPT assessed value and to
reconcile the balances of the RPT reciprocal account and contra account RPT
at year end. Effect the necessary adjustments in order to reflect accurate and
reliable balances in the Financial Statements.

We reiterated the recommendation that the Municipal Mayor advise the
Municipal Accountant to initiate action in cleaning the dormant receivables in
the books of account in accordance with COA Circular 2016-005. Also, direct
the Municipal Accountant and other official concerned to:
a. comply with COA Circular 2016-005 to accurately reflect reliable amount of
receivables in the Financial Statements;
b. consider requesting for authority to write-off dormant receivables subject
to the conditions set forth by the above Circular and submit to the Auditor
for review and evaluation;
c. set up Allowance for Impairment for recognition of possible losses and
effect the adjustment in the amount of asset reflected in the Financial
Statements for fair presentation; and
d. continue to exert effort for its collectability when circumstances would
warrant.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. direct the Municipal Accountant to maintain property ledger cards to
support the amount reflected in the general ledger to ascertain the
completeness of the Inventory balances reported in the Financial
Statements; and
b. require the General Service Officer to maintain stock cards to fully account
and validate the receipts and issuances of Inventories; and to conduct
inventory taking and prepare Report of Physical Count of Inventory for
reconciliation with the Property and Accounting records.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to
coordinate with the Provincial Accountant and reconcile the balance of Due
from LGUs account appearing in the books of the Municipality against the
balance of Due to LGUs account appearing in the records of the Provincial
Government. Effect the necessary adjustment upon reconciliation to correct
the discrepancy to arrive at an accurate and correct balance of the account in
the Financial Statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to
draw the necessary Journal Entry Voucher to derecognize/drop the small value
property items from the books by debiting Prior Years’ Adjustment and
crediting the PPE accounts in the books in the amount of P234,027.51.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Municipal Accountant comply with the reporting guidelines on Local Roads
Asset Management System as prescribed under COA Circular No. 2015-008
and maintain Subsidiary Ledger and provide full disclosures of the details
of Road Network in the Notes to Financial Statements using the prescribed
format ;
b. Municipal Engineer to provide the Municipal Accountant and Municipal
General Services Officer with the complete description and cost
segregation of road components for road projects;
c. MGSO to keep complete Road Network Property Cards.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the MDRRMO Property
Officer to prepare the Inventory List of Procured/Donated Items for DRRM and
submit the same to the Audit Team for monitoring in compliance with COA
Circular No. 2014-02.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Administrator,
and the General Services Officer to facilitate the titling of subject lots as well
as the transfer of title still in process to avoid contingent claims and disputes
that may arise in the future in compliance with Section 148 of COA Circular No.
92-386 and Section 47 of the Implementing Rules and Regulations (IRR) of
the Local Government Code of 1991.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal General
Services Officer to prepare the list of insurable property and facilitate the
filing/application for the necessary insurance coverage of all these assets with
the GSIS under the General Insurance Fund as required under RA No. 656, as
amended and COA Circular No. 92-390 dated November 17, 1992.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. through the Sangguniang Bayan, establish specific guidelines, aligned with
DSWD MC No. 11 series of 2019, in the granting of financial assistance to
institutionalize a uniform schedule, consistent, objective system of
operation in responding to cases of individuals and families in crisis
situation; and
b. direct the Accountant to submit to the Auditor the documents used as
basis for payment of Financial Assistance to beneficiaries listed in
Appendix M in order to prove the regularity of payment.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to
adopt measures and strategies that will intensify and maximize collection of
taxes. Likewise, avail of remedies and sanctions as provided in RA No. 7160
for strict imposition of collection that will generate income and increase the
sources of fund of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer
and the Municipal Accountant to release the barangays’ shares from RPT
within five days after the end of each quarter as required under Section 271(d)
of RA No. 7160.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant
to ensure that disbursement vouchers are processed and paid with complete
documentation to established the propriety of expenses paid in compliance
Section 9.1.3.2 of COA Circular 2012-001.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant
to submit immediately the lacking documents for the paid Disbursement
Vouchers amounting to P10,057,593.89 in compliance with Section 4(6) of
Presidential Decree No. 1445 and Section 9.1.3.2 of the Commission on Audit
(COA) Circular No. 2012-001.

We recommended that the Municipal Engineer provide the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal General Services Officer (MGSO) with the complete description and cost segregation of the road components for the road projects to facilitate the proper disclosure of the road network system in the Notes to Financial Statements by the Municipal Accountant and proper maintenance of Local Road Inventory, Road Map and Local Road Network Property Card by the MGSO.
observations.managementAction: There is an on-going preparation of the reports.
observations.reason: Reports on the information, description and cost segregation of the road components for the road projects are not completed.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to draw a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) debiting RPT Receivable/SET Receivable and crediting Deferred RPT Receivables/Deferred SET Income based on the certified list of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year provided by the Municipal Treasurer, for fair presentation of the accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Copy of JEV No. 100-2019-01-004 and 200-2019-01-0001 were prepared indicating setting up of the correct RPT and SET.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor advise the Municipal Accountant to analyze and evaluate the collectability of the long outstanding receivables of P2,481,566.00. A request for write-off of dormant accounts subject to the conditions provided under COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 16, 2016 may be considered. Also, set up appropriate Allowance for Impairment for the possible losses.
observations.managementAction: The Management has yet to finish the collection analysis of the long outstanding receivables. A request for write-off of dormant accounts subject to the conditions provided under COA Circular No. 2016-005 may be considered.
observations.reason: There is no collection made yet from these long outstanding receivables and only initiated the creation of a committee that will review the status of the receivables for request of write-off.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal General Services Officer to maintain Stock Cards and conduct actual inventory of its accounts and prepare and submit the RPCI to the Audit Team, as required under Sections 119 and 124 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I, respectively.
observations.managementAction: There is an on-going preparation of Stock Cards. The Municipal Accountant has started updating its property ledger cards. The MGSO is using the General Services Management System (GSMS) software but not fully operational due to some minor errors to be improved by the service provider; for the time being, the Office will resort to maintenance of stock cards as recommended.
observations.reason: Preparation and updating of stock cards are on-going and not completed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the Provincial Accountant of Laguna to address the discrepancies in the reciprocal accounts Due from LGUs and Due to LGUs and make the necessary adjustments.
observations.managementAction: The Management coordinated with the Provincial Government of Laguna regarding the discrepancies in the reciprocal accounts.
observations.reason: There is no adjustment made yet. The Management has not furnished the MOA/Trust Agreement/MOU for the utilization and disposition of funds; reconciliation is still to be done.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Administrator, Municipal Assessor and Municipal General Services Officer (MGSO) to facilitate the transfer of title of the remaining lots in the name of the Municipality to support its claims of ownership and to protect the interest of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal General Services Officer, the Municipal Assessor and the Municipal Legal Officer are already exerting efforts to establish the proper ownership of land owned by the Municipality. A photocopy of the Sangguniang Bayan (SB) Resolution which approved the transfer of School Land Sites was submitted to the Auditor’s Office for reference.
observations.reason: Processing of titling will take a lot of time since there is a need to locate the documents such as the Deed of Donations and other related legal documents before processing the same.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the immediate submission of the required documents, in compliance with Section 4 of PD No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 2012-001.
observations.managementAction: The required documents for the Infrastructure projects were submitted.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct the LCPC to fully utilize the appropriated amount for LCPC and implement fully the planned PPAs, in compliance with RA No. 9344 and DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-120. Also recommended regular monitoring and closer coordination to ensure projects are implemented and completed on time.
observations.managementAction: There is an on-going implementation of activities and LCPC Programs. Quarterly Reports were prepared to monitor the status of the projects.
observations.reason: The remaining programs, projects and activities are still on-going and expected to be implemented and completed before the year end.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the concerned officials accomplish/execute the MOA/Trust Agreement and/or agreement for the utilization of funds to assist the Management in the utilization and disposition of any balance thereof upon completion.
observations.managementAction: The Management requested for the copies of MOAs and/or agreements but the Management has not yet furnished the documents to the Audit Team.
observations.reason: No documents were available.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor cause the full utilization and implementation of the planned projects for the 20 per cent Development Fund so that the desired services and benefits can be delivered to the constituents of the Municipality and achieve desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes. Regular monitoring and closer coordination among officials involved in planning, execution and monitoring should be undertaken.
observations.managementAction: There is on-going implementation of the projects. The Management cited reasons for non-implementation and indicated projects to be completed early in the ensuing year.
observations.reason: The remaining programs, projects and activities are still on-going and expected to be implemented and completed in the early part of the ensuing year.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the GAD Focal Person to submit the Municipality’s Annual Accomplishment Report with the required attachments such as: (a) brief summary of the reported program or project; (b) copies of reported policy issuances; (c) results of Harmonized Gender Development Guidelines (HGDG) tests, if any; and (d) actions taken by the LGU on the COA audit findings and recommendations.
observations.managementAction: The GAD Focal Person submitted the Annual Accomplishment Report and provided required attachments such as brief summaries, pictures and attendance sheets to support the conducted GAD related activities.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct the GAD Focal Point Person to establish a GAD database in compliance with Item 4.1 B of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA to serve as basis in budgeting and planning GAD PPAs which are responsive to the gender issues in the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The plan of data gathering was started. The GAD Focal Person assured the full establishment of GAD Database in the ensuing year.
observations.reason: Data gathering was started but not completed, thus the GAD Database is still not established by the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council (LDRRMC) to ensure that PPAs embodied in the LDRRMP are fully implemented to protect the community and lessen the impact of disasters, in compliance with DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-73. Also recommended reconciliation of reports submitted by MPDC, Budget Department and Accounting Department on utilization of the LDRRMF.
observations.managementAction: The Management reconciled the reports submitted by the MPDC, Budget Department and Accounting Department on the utilization of the Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund.
observations.reason: Full implementation of the remaining programs, projects and activities for the LDRRMF is still on-going and expected at year end and early part of the ensuing year.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Barangay Officials to look for conducive locations for the Material Recovery Facilities so that all barangays have MRFs, in compliance with Section 1, Rule XI of the IRR of RA No. 9003 and DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2009-168; instruct Barangay Officials to include a composting operation in the MRFs and ensure the MRFs are fully operating and functioning; and that the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board monitor and inspect the condition and operation of established Barangay MRFs as required under Section 4(b) and (c), Rule VI of the IRR of RA No. 9003.
observations.managementAction: The designated MENRO informed the Audit Team that two Barangays are processing the establishment of its own MRF. The audit observation was coordinated with the Association of Barangay Chairman President for implementation and necessary action of the concerned Barangays. The Management noted the recommendation on the absence of Multi-Purpose Cooperatives/Association and committed to possibility of creating a Multi-Purpose Cooperative. Updating of the Ten Year Waste Management Plan is included in the Agenda of the 2020 1st quarter meeting conducted by the Municipal Ecological Solid Waste Management Board held on February 5, 2020.
observations.reason: Not all Barangays have MRF that is fully operational. Some Barangays are still looking for conducive locations for MRFs.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Engineer provide the Municipal
Accountant and MGSO with the complete description and cost segregation of
road components for road projects to facilitate the proper disclosure of the
road network system in the Notes to Financial Statements by the Municipal
Accountant and proper maintenance of Local Road Inventory, Road Map and
Local Road Network Property Card by the Municipal General Services Officer.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct the
Municipal Accountant to draw a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) debiting RPT
Receivable/SET Receivable and crediting Deferred RPT Income/Deferred SET
Income based on the certified list of taxpayers and the amount due and
collectible for the year provided by the Municipal Treasurer, for fair
presentation of the accountsin the financial statements.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor advise the Municipal
Accountant to analyze and evaluate further the collectability of the
long outstanding receivables of P2,481,566.00. A request for write-off of
dormant accounts subject to the conditions provided under COA Circular No.
2016-005 dated December 16, 2016 may be considered. Also, set up appropriate
Allowance for Impairment for the possible losses.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal General
Services Officer to maintain Stock Cards and conduct actual inventory of its
Inventory accounts and prepare and submit the RPCI to the Audit Team, as
required under Sections 119 and 124 of the MNGAS for LGU, Volume I,
respectively.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to
coordinate with the Provincial Accountant of Laguna to address the
discrepancies in the reciprocal accounts Due from LGUs and Due to LGUs and
make the necessary adjustments.

We reiterated our previous recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct
the Municipal Administrator, Municipal Assessor and Municipal General
Services Officer (MGSO) to facilitate the transfer of title of the remaining lots in
the name of the Municipality of Santa Cruz to support its claims of ownership and
to protect the interest of the Municipality.

We recommended the immediate submission of the required documents, in
compliance with Section 4 of PD No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 2012-001.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct the LCPC to
fully utilize the appropriated amount for LCPC and implement fully the planned
PPAs, in compliance with RA No. 9344 and DILG Memorandum Circular No.
2012-120.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct the
concerned officials to accomplish/execute the MOA/Trust Agreement/MOU for
the above mentioned fund transfers to assist management in the utilization of
funds and disposition of any balance thereof upon completion of the purpose
of the funds transferred.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal General Services Officer (MGSO) to request the disposal of unserviceable properties through the Audit Team Leader to avoid the further deterioration of the unserviceable properties.
observations.managementAction: The Management requested the disposal of unserviceable properties.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to refrain from directly recording the procurement or receipt of supplies and materials to expense account, but instead adopt the Perpetual Inventory Method of accounting and record issuances using the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) submitted by the Municipal General Services Officer as basis; and Municipal General Services Officer (MGSO) to maintain supplies ledger cards for each item of supplies and materials purchased/acquired and submit monthly the SSMI to the Municipal Accountant for proper recording of the Supplies and Materials Expense accounts.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant has already adopted the Perpetual Inventory Method in recording the purchase of supplies and materials. The MGSO is submitting the SSMI to the Office of Municipal Accountant.
observations.reason: The MGSO was not able to maintain the supplies ledger cards.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our previous recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Administrator, Municipal Assessor and Municipal General Services Officer (MGSO) to facilitate the transfer of title of the remaining lots in the name of the Municipality of Santa Cruz to support its claims of ownership and to protect the interest of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The Management was able to transfer some of the land titles in the name of Municipality.
observations.reason: Not all land titles were transferred in the name of the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to draw a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to reverse the entry made in writing off loan payable and write off the account only after full payment of the principal balance.
observations.managementAction: The outstanding principal loan balance was already fully paid and necessary adjustments were made by the Accountant.

observations.recommendation: We also recommended the immediate submission of the lacking documents to the Audit Team to facilitate the determination of the propriety, regularity and validity of the claims.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality submitted the copy of the warranty security.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to draw a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) debiting Real Property Tax Receivable/Special Education Tax Receivable and crediting Deferred Real Property Tax Income/Deferred Special Education Tax Income based on the list provided by the Municipal Treasurer, for fair presentation of the accounts in the financial statements; We also recommended the concerned officials to expedite the review and clean-up of the Municipality's assessment roll through thorough tax mapping in order to come up with a more accurate and reliable certified list of taxpayers as basis of setting up Real Property Tax and Special Education Tax Receivables of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant furnished the Audit Team with a copy of JEV No. 100-2019-01-0004 and No. 200-2019-01-0001 both dated January 3, 2019 indicating the setting up of the correct amount of RPT/SET Receivables.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor advise the Municipal Accountant to evaluate the collectability of the overdue loans receivable, otherwise take appropriate action to cleanse the books of dormant accounts.
observations.managementAction: The Management created a committee for the review and assessment of the outstanding loan receivables.
observations.reason: No collection was received during the year.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to disclose the required information in the Notes to Financial Statement to assist the users in understanding the financial statements and to provide them with additional information value.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant was able to present some of the necessary information in the disclosures in the Notes to the Financial Statements.
observations.reason: There are still undisclosed statements.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Local Council for the Protection of Children (LCPC) to fully utilize the budget for the LCPC and submit the required reports annually to the Office of the Auditor including Council’s Annual Work and Financial Plan; Local Development Plan for Children; Local Code for Children; Local State of Children’s Report; Local Investment Plan for Children; and Accomplishment Reports.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality was able to submit the lacking documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor request the Sangguniang Bayan to enact an Ordinance that would provide the official guidelines in the granting of medical/financial assistance to qualified beneficiaries.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality adopted the policy of DSWD for granting of the financial assistance.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct the concerned officials to accomplish/execute the MOA/Trust Agreement/MOU for the fund transfers to assist management in the utilization of funds and disposition of any balance thereof upon completion of the purpose of the funds transferred.
observations.managementAction: Copy of some agreements was submitted to the Audit Team for review.
observations.reason: There are still transfers from NGAs not covered with MOA or any agreements.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor cause the full utilization and implementation of the planned projects for the 20% Development Fund so that the desired services and benefits can be delivered to the constituents of the Municipality and help achieve desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes. We also recommended that regular monitoring mechanisms be employed in assessing the rate of progress of project implementation and that a closer coordination among the officials involved in planning, execution and monitoring be undertaken to ensure that programmed projects are implemented and completed on time. Lastly, we recommended the reconciliation of the reports submitted by the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC) and Budget Department on the utilization of the 20% Development Fund.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality has implemented some of the planned development projects. The reports were reconciled by the MPDC and Budget Departments.
observations.reason: The Municipality still was not able to maximize the utilization of 20% Development Fund.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct the GAD Focal Point Person to establish a GAD database in compliance with Item 4.1 B of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01 to serve as basis in budgeting and planning GAD PPAs which are responsive to the gender issues in the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality started gathering data for the establishment of GAD Database.
observations.reason: The GAD Database is still not established by the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct the MDRRMC to monitor the maximum utilization of the Mitigation Fund to better prepare and lessen the impact of deadly and costly natural and man-made hazards and disasters. We recommended the reconciliation of the reports submitted by the MPDC and Budget Department/Accounting Department on the utilization of the Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality has implemented some of the planned programs, projects and activities. The CY 2017 reports were reconciled by the MPDC and Budget Departments.
observations.reason: The Municipality was not able to fully utilize the LDRRMF.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to transfer the unexpended/unobligated balance of the Mitigation Fund-MOOE from the General Fund to the Special Trust Fund of the Trust Fund Books.
observations.managementAction: The unexpended/unobligated balance of the Mitigation Fund-MOOE was already transferred from General Fund to the Special Trust Fund book.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our previous year's audit recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct the GAD Focal Point Person to establish a GAD database in compliance with Item 4.1 B of JMC No. 2013-01 of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA to serve as basis in budgeting and planning GAD PPAs.
observations.managementAction: The Management has started gathering information as basis for GAD Database.
observations.reason: The Municipality’s GAD database has not yet established.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to transfer the unexpended/unobligated balance of the Mitigation Fund-MOOE in the Trust Liabilities-DRRMF to the Trust Fund books, in compliance with COA Circular No. 2012-002 by preparing the corresponding adjusting entries to correct the affected accounts both in the General and Trust Fund Books.
observations.managementAction: Only Quick Response Fund were transferred in the Trust Fund.
observations.reason: The unexpended balance of the Mitigation Fund-MOOE was not yet transferred to Trust Fund. Reiterated in observation No. 17 under Part II of the report.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct
the MGSO to request the disposal of the unserviceable properties through
the Audit Team Leader to avoid the further deterioration of the
unserviceable properties.

We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that the
Municipal Mayor direct:

* the Municipal Accountant to refrain from directly recording the

procurement or receipt of supplies and materials to expense
account, but instead adopt the Perpetual Inventory Method of
accounting and record issuances using the SSMI submitted by the Municipal General Services Officer as basis; and

e the MGSO to maintain supplies ledger cards for each item of
supplies and materials purchased/acquired and submit monthly
the SSMI to the Municipal Accountant for proper recording of the
Supplies and Materials Expense accounts.

We reiterated our previous recommendation that the Municipal
Mayor direct the Municipal Administrator, Municipal Assessor and
Municipal GSO to facilitate the transfer of title of the remaining lots in the
name of the Municipality of Santa Cruz to support its claims of ownership
and to protect the interest of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipality communicate with the
Provincial Government regarding the status of the request for billing or
Statement of Account to process the settlement of the loan.

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal
Accountant to draw Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to reverse the entry made in
writing off the interest payable and to write-off the account only after full
payment of principal balance has been made.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant require the posting
of warranty security by the Contractors for infrastructure projects to ensure
that the Contractors will faithfully perform their responsibilities.

We also recommended the immediate submission of the lacking
documents to the Audit Team to facilitate the determination of the propriety,
regularity and validity of the claims.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal
Accountant to draw a Journal Entry Voucher debiting Real Property Tax
Receivable/Special Education Tax Receivable and crediting Deferred Real
Property Tax Income/Deferred Special Education Tax Income based on the
list provided by the Municipal Treasurer, for fair presentation of the
accounts in the financial statements.

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the concerned
officials to expedite the review and clean up of the Municipality's assessment
roll through thorough tax mapping in order to come up with a more accurate
and reliable certified list of taxpayers as basis of setting up Real Property
Tax and Special Education Tax Receivables of the Municipality.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor advise
the Municipal Accountant to evaluate the collectability of the overdue loans
receivable, otherwise appropriate action should be taken to cleanse the books
of dormant accounts.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct
the Municipal Accountant to disclose the required information in the Notes to
Financial Statement to assist the users in understanding the financial
statements and to provide them with additional information value.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor request the Sangguniang
Bayan to enact an Ordinance that would provide the official guidelines in the
granting of medical/financial assistance to qualified beneficiaries.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the concerned
officials to accomplish/execute the Memorandums of Agreement
(MOA)/Trust Agreement/Memorandum of Understanding for the above
mentioned fund transfers to assist management in the utilization of funds
and disposition of any balance thereof upon completion of the purpose of the
funds transferred.
