We  recommended | that the Municipal Mayor | direct the Municipal | Accountant and | Municipal General Services | Officer (MGSO) to | fast track the | reconciliation of the | PPE accounts’ | balance per books and | RPCPPE and make | the necessary | adjustments, | We also | recommended that the | Municipal Mayor, to | avoid the further | deterioration of the | unserviceable | properties, direct the | MGSO to: | a) request the disposal | of the unserviceable | properties; and | b) dispose the | unserviceable | properties upon | approval of the request. | In addition, require | the Municipal | Accountant to | disclose in the Notes | to Financial | Statements the | unserviceable | properties, pending | disposal.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant and the MGSO already reconciled their records.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our prior year’s audit recommendation that the Municipal Mayor: * direct the Municipal Accountant to stop using the Direct Expense Method in accounting for the receipt and issuances of supplies and materials.  Adopt instead the Perpetual Inventory Method in compliance with COA Circular No. 2015-009 and Sections 4(j) and 120 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I; and * require the MGSO to summarize the issuances of supplies and materials during the month in the SSMI and submit the same to the Municipal Accountant to serve as basis in debiting the corresponding supplies and material expense accounts.
observations.managementAction: The Accountant partially used the Direct Expense Method. The MGSO submitted quarterly the SSML Reiterated in observation No. 2 under Part II of the report.
observations.reason: The MGSO was not able to prepare the SSMI in a monthly basis.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor prioritize the discussion with the concerned officials of the Provincial Government of Laguna on the status of their request for condonation of the loans payable.
observations.managementAction: The Management has communicated to the Provincial Government regarding the status of the condonation of the loans payable.
observations.reason: The Municipality has not yet paid the loan balance. Reiterated in observation No. 4 under Part II of the report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to book up the Grasscutters and Computer Software in accordance with the Revised Chart of Accounts for LGUs.
observations.managementAction: The Accountant reclassified already accounts.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our prior year's audit recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Administrator, Municipal Assessor and MGSO to facilitate the transfer of titles of the remaining 36 parcels of land in the name of the Municipal Government of Santa Cruz to protect the interest of the municipality.
observations.managementAction: Transfer of some Land titles had been facilitated by Municipality.
observations.reason: There are still parcels of land that have no TCTs. Reiterated in observation No. 3 under Part II of the report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Municipal Mayor direct the Local Finance Committee to exercise utmost diligence in the disposition of government funds to avoid the incurrence of cash deficit.
observations.managementAction: The Finance Committee exercise utmost diligence in the disposition of government funds to avoid the incurrence of cash deficit.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the concerned officials to submit immediately to the Audit Team the required documents, otherwise, the transactions shall be suspended/disallowed in audit.
observations.managementAction: The concerned officials submitted the required documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to draw a JEV and take up the adjusting entry in the Trust Fund books.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant had drawn a JEV to book up the adjusting entry.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor advise the Municipal Accountant to evaluate the collectibility of the overdue loans receivable, otherwise, appropriate action should be taken to cleanse the books of dormant accounts.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality implemented projects under the 20% Municipal Development Fund. Reiterated in observation No. 7 under Part II of the report.
observations.reason: There are still uncollected overdue loans receivable.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to review all the accounting entries that affect both accounts, determine the cause/s of the discrepancy between their balances, after which make the necessary adjustments to bring into agreement the reciprocal accounts, thus ensuring their fair presentation in the Statement of Financial Condition.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant had drawn a JEV to book up the adjusting entry. Reiterated in observation No. 7 under Part II of the report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to disclose the required information in the Notes to Financial Statements to assist the users in understanding the financial statements and to provide them with additional information value.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant implemented information disclosed in the Notes to Financial Statements. The Municipality implemented projects under the 20% Municipal Development Fund.
observations.reason: Some necessary information remained not disclosed in the Notes to Financial Statements. Reiterated in observation No. 8 under Part II of the report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator to determine and address the causes of the inability to optimally utilize the 20% Municipal Development Fund and fully implement the projects funded out of the fund within the timeline indicated in the Annual Investment Plan.
observations.managementAction: The Management monitored the implementation of the projects under the 20% Municipal Development Fund.
observations.reason: There are projects that still unimplemented. Reiterated in observation No. 12 under Part II of the report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor strengthen its LGU GFPS by adding representatives from Indigenous People (IPs), Persons with Disabilities (PWDs), private sector and women's association from the LCE’s office in its Execom to ensure that GAD PPAs are comprehensive enough to address the existing gender issues in the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: LGU GFPS has been reconstituted to comply with the requirements.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the GAD Focal Person to ensure that GAD Fund is utilized only for purposes for which it was created. Enforce strict adherence to JMC No. 2013-01 of the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA.
observations.managementAction: GAD funds were utilized for GAD related PPAs.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction Management Council (MDRRMC) to monitor the maximum utilization of the Mitigation Fund to better prepare and lessen the impact of deadly and costly natural and man-made hazards and disasters.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality implemented projects under the LDRRMF.
observations.reason: The LDRRMF was not fully utilized.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to comply with the disclosure requirement for the LDRRMF per COA Circular No. 2012-002.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant complied with the disclosure requirements.
